entries.title: Test suite red blocks structural review
Test helper re-exports stdlib it does not use, generating pervasive lint noise
make_args() has no contract-sync test — will drift again on next arg addition
PTH108 + I001 lint violations in run_review.py (pre-existing)
run_review() 405-LoC flat function body with no sub-function structure

finding_snapshot.title: run_review() 405-LoC flat function body with no sub-function structure
finding_snapshot.why_it_matters: run_review() is a large coordinating function; the two-attempt loop + codex-setup phases remain inline.
finding_snapshot.what_is_wrong: The remaining phases thread use_resume/session_id/fallback_used/returncode in+out, so extracting them adds ceremony.
finding_snapshot.why_weakens_submission: A 9-anchor structure would have every cohesive phase behind a named helper; the residual flat region holds simplicity below the anchor.
finding_snapshot.minimal_correction_path: Accept the inline phases as irreducible, OR pursue the marginal CodexCapture value-return.

finding_snapshot.title: run_review() flat body — residual phases (irreducible without ceremony)
finding_snapshot.why_it_matters: After loop 5's three extractions, run_review() is 330 LoC. The two-attempt loop and codex-setup phases thread use_resume/session_id/fallback_used/returncode in+out and reads them post-loop, making extraction add ceremony.
finding_snapshot.what_is_wrong: Four inout locals thread through any candidate helper at run_review.py:397-533 + :386-389.
finding_snapshot.why_weakens_submission: simplicity (6.5) and arch (7) cannot honestly reach 9.5 accepted residual; 9-anchor not met.
finding_snapshot.minimal_correction_path: CodexCapture namedtuple deepening candidate (removes one inout pair from codex-setup phase, marginal improvement).

entries.title: make_args() dict still manually maintained — two-step edit requirement persists

finding_snapshot.title: make_args() dict still manually maintained — two-step edit requirement persists
finding_snapshot.why_it_matters: make_args() restated the run_review.py CLI arg contract and defaults in a hand-maintained 17-key dict; the next arg addition required a two-step edit and caused the loop-1 build failure.
finding_snapshot.what_is_wrong: scripts/tests/_helpers.py:make_args() carried a parallel dict of arg names + defaults instead of deriving them from parse_args().
finding_snapshot.why_weakens_submission: Duplicated authority over the arg-name set is a drift hazard that already produced a real build failure.
finding_snapshot.minimal_correction_path: Derive make_args() defaults from run_review.parse_args() under a saved/restored sys.argv; delete the dict and the now-unused argparse import.

entries.title: README documents a test command that collects zero tests + a stale test count

finding_snapshot.title: README documents a test command that collects zero tests + a stale test count
finding_snapshot.why_it_matters: A new contributor (or an AI agent) following README.md to run the suite is told to run 'cd scripts && python3 -m pytest test_run_review.py test_web_search.py' (README.md:52) against files described as the pytest suite (README.md:41-42). One of those files does not exist and the other is a unittest discovery shim, so the documented command collects zero tests.
finding_snapshot.what_is_wrong: README.md:42 references scripts/test_web_search.py which does not exist. README.md:41 claims 'test_run_review.py pytest suite (118 tests)' — stale count (actual 119) and mislabeled. The canonical command is 'python3 -m pytest scripts/tests/'.
finding_snapshot.why_weakens_submission: A documented entrypoint that silently collects zero tests is a doc-vs-code credibility leak per method.md Step 6.
evidence: README.md:41 'test_run_review.py  pytest suite (118 tests)' — stale count (actual 119) + mislabel
README.md:42 'test_web_search.py  web-search adapter pytest suite' — file does not exist
README.md:52 'cd scripts && python3 -m pytest test_run_review.py test_web_search.py' — collects zero tests
finding_snapshot.minimal_correction_path: Update README.md to document the canonical command 'python3 -m pytest scripts/tests/'; correct the test count to 119; drop the test_web_search.py reference.

finding_snapshot.title: README documents a test command that collects zero tests + a stale test count
finding_snapshot.why_it_matters: README.md documented 'cd scripts && python3 -m pytest test_run_review.py test_web_search.py' — test_web_search.py absent; test_run_review.py is a unittest shim; command collected zero tests.
finding_snapshot.what_is_wrong: README.md:42 referenced non-existent test_web_search.py; README.md:41 claimed 118 tests (actual 119); README.md:52 had the stale/broken command.
finding_snapshot.why_weakens_submission: Doc-vs-code credibility leak: new contributors would conclude the project is untested.
finding_snapshot.minimal_correction_path: Applied: dropped test_web_search.py reference, relabeled tests/, corrected count and command.

entries.title: Test-lint star-import pattern: 187 violations in scripts/tests/ (F405/RUF100/I001/E401)

finding_snapshot.title: Test-lint star-import pattern: 187 violations in scripts/tests/
finding_snapshot.why_it_matters: python3 -m ruff check scripts/tests/ -> 187 errors: F405 x154 / RUF100 x19 / I001 x7 / E401 x7 from 'from ._helpers import *' in test_*.py files. Caps framework_idioms at 6.5.
finding_snapshot.what_is_wrong: All 7 test files use 'from ._helpers import *' for test ergonomics. Ruff flags F405 (undefined names from star import) and related violations pervasively.
finding_snapshot.why_weakens_submission: framework_idioms cannot honestly score above 6.5 with 187 standing lint violations in the test scope.
evidence: python3 -m ruff check scripts/tests/ -> Found 187 errors (verified loop 7)
finding_snapshot.minimal_correction_path: Replace 'from ._helpers import *' with explicit imports across 7 test files. SPT Q2 fails: ceremony, no behavior change, no contest-architecture gain.
finding_snapshot.spt_disposition: SPT Q2 rejected (ceremony with no behavior/architecture gain). Accepted as deliberate ergonomic pattern. Expires 2026-12-22.
